title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme - Open
snippet: Published on January 7, 2026, this news article discusses a research study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino. The study, which analyzed over 2.5 million Facebook posts, concludes that Facebook's algorithm penalizes institutional political content while boosting anti-European and extremist voices.
description: This news article discusses a recent study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, which concludes that Facebook's algorithm favors anti-European extremist content over official political discourse.

title: Katharina Esau Was Awarded an ARC Discovery Early Career Researcher Award 2026 - Oncodaily
ICA 2026 Conference Program
snippet: Fabio Giglietto is listed as a co-author and presenter at the International Communication Association (ICA) 2026 conference. He is participating in a session titled "Communication and Inequalities in Context" with a paper "Clicking Politics on an Algorithmic Feed: Political Efficacy and Longitudinal Information. Selection."
description: Fabio Giglietto is listed as a co-author and presenter at the International Communication Association (ICA) 2026 conference, presenting a paper titled "Clicking Politics on an Algorithmic Feed: Political Efficacy and Longitudinal Information. Selection."

Full list of Panels 2026 - International Conference on Social Media & Society
snippet: Fabio Giglietto is listed as a panelist for the "Meta Content Library Community Roundtable – Where to for Social Media Research?" at the International Conference on Social Media & Society (SMSociety) 2026, where leading social media researchers discuss their experiences with the Meta Content Library.
description: The International Conference on Social Media & Society (SMSociety) 2026 lists Fabio Giglietto as a panelist for the "Meta Content Library Community Roundtable – Where to for Social Media Research?" session.
